verdict_reasoning: SRC is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically localized to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic face. However, SRC is well-documented to undergo cell-state-induced and oncogenic-transformation-dependent surface translocation. In transformed and tumor cells, SRC displays on the outer leaflet ('ecto-SRC') and is recognized by antibodies and targeted therapeutically. SRC also cycles transiently to the PM during signaling and is recruited to receptor complexes. These non-baseline surface pools qualify as contextual via cell_state_induced (transformation-associated ecto-display) and dual_localization (minor PM cycling pools).
